title: EEG: DPX Cog Ctl Task in Acute Mild TBI
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from a Dot Probe Continuous Performance Task administered to control subjects and individuals with sub-acute mild traumatic brain injury (mTBI). Participants were assessed at multiple timepoints: within 3-14 days post-injury (Session 1), approximately 2 months post-injury (Session 2), and approximately 4 months post-injury (Session 3). The dataset includes behavioral and electrophysiological measures of attention and cognitive control relevant to understanding post-injury recovery trajectories.
methods_description: EEG data were collected during a Dot Probe Continuous Performance Task using Matlab programming. Data collection occurred between 2016-2018 at the Center for Brain Recovery and Repair at the UNM Health Sciences Center. Participants with mTBI were assessed at three sessions: Session 1 (3-14 days post-injury), Session 2 (~1.5-3 months post-injury), and Session 3 (~3-5 months post-injury). Control subjects were assessed at corresponding timepoints.
